an1.296:1.1: "One *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to complete disenchantment, to dispassion, to cessation, to stilling, to direct knowledge, to enlightenment, to *nibbāna*.
an1.296:1.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is that one *dhamma* which, when developed and cultivated, leads to complete disenchantment, to dispassion, to cessation, to stilling, to direct knowledge, to enlightenment, to *nibbāna*."
an1.297-305:1.1: "One *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, when developed and cultivated, leads to complete disenchantment, to dispassion, to cessation, to stilling, to direct knowledge, to enlightenment, to *nibbāna*.
an1.297-305:1.3: Recollection of the *Dhamma* … recollection of the *Saṅgha* … recollection of virtue … recollection of generosity … recollection of *devatās* … *sati* of breathing … *sati* of death … *sati* directed to the body … recollection of stilling.
an1.297-305:1.4: This, *bhikkhus*, is that one *dhamma* which, when developed and cultivated, leads to complete disenchantment, to dispassion, to cessation, to stilling, to direct knowledge, to enlightenment, to *nibbāna*."
